verses.text: ‘He, that being often reproved hardeneth his neck, shall suddenly be destroyed.’ The Hebrew qasheh oref — hard of neck — evokes a stubborn animal that refuses to turn under the yoke. The repeated refusal of correction has consequences. The proverb is sober: spiritual hardness usually shatters not gradually but all at once, when something finally breaks.

When the righteous lead, the people rejoice; when the wicked rule, the people mourn. The Hebrew anach — to sigh, groan — names a sustained communal grief. Leadership shapes the air a society breathes. The verse is a quiet political theology: the moral character of those in power affects the joy or grief of the whole nation.
La sabiduría de un hombre impacta a sus padres ( **10:1,** **15:20,** **17:21** ). • Las prostitutas son costosas y la riqueza de un hombre se dilapida rápidamente con ellas.\n\n
Un rey justo protege los derechos de los demás; un gobernante que acepta sobornos utiliza su cargo para beneficio personal (véase también **17:23,** **22:16,** **28:21** ).\n\n
La adulación a menudo es una trampa para aprovecharse de alguien ( **7:21-22,** **26:23** ).\n\n
